perturbation.original_text: 1. Grant of Rights 1.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Todos hereby grants the Reseller a non-sublicensable, non-transferable, exclusive right to distribute and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory; provided, however, that Reseller may sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate of the Reseller. The Reseller shall have a right of first refusal to include within this Agreement any additional products developed, manufactured, or sold by the Company following the Effective Date that are not currently included in Exhibit A, and upon the exercise of such right, the term "Products" shall be expanded to mean such additional products as well. For purposes of clarity, the parties agree that upon Todos's development of a blood screening test for colon cancer, such product shall be added to this Agreement and included within the definition of "Products", subject to the Reseller and Todos agreeing on the commercial terms for such product, including the price.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Grant of Rights 1.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Todos hereby grants the Reseller a non-sublicensable, non-transferable, exclusive right to distribute and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory; Reseller may not sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate of the Reseller. The Reseller shall have a right of first refusal to include within this Agreement any additional products developed, manufactured, or sold by the Company following the Effective Date that are not currently included in Exhibit A, and upon the exercise of such right, the term "Products" shall be expanded to mean such additional products as well. For purposes of clarity, the parties agree that upon Todos's development of a blood screening test for colon cancer, such product shall be added to this Agreement and included within the definition of "Products", subject to the Reseller and Todos agreeing on the commercial terms for such product, including the price.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 1.1 grants the Reseller the right to sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate. The changed text removes the phrase 'provided, however, that Reseller may sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate of the Reseller.' This creates a direct contradiction, as the Reseller is initially granted this right and is then prohibited from doing so in the same sentence, creating an in-text contradiction and making enforcement unclear.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Grant of Rights 1.1 Subject to the terms and conditions of this Agreement, Todos hereby grants the Reseller a non-sublicensable, non-transferable, exclusive right to distribute and sell the Products to Customers in the Territory; Reseller may not sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate of the Reseller. The Reseller shall have a right of first refusal to include within this Agreement any additional products developed, manufactured, or sold by the Company following the Effective Date that are not currently included in Exhibit A, and upon the exercise of such right, the term "Products" shall be expanded to mean such additional products as well. For purposes of clarity, the parties agree that upon Todos's development of a blood screening test for colon cancer, such product shall be added to this Agreement and included within the definition of "Products", subject to the Reseller and Todos agreeing on the commercial terms for such product, including the price.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 1.1 removes the reseller's right to sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate, which was originally permitted. This directly contradicts the original agreement by explicitly forbidding what was previously allowed within the same clause. This is a strong in-text contradiction as the reseller is both allowed and disallowed the right to sub-license or transfer its distribution rights to a subsidiary or affiliate.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Term and Termination 11.1 This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a period of five (5) years from the Reseller's first purchase order for Product issued to Todos (the "Initial Term"), unless terminated earlier by one of the parties in accordance with the terms of this Section 11. Upon completion of the Initial Term, provided that the Reseller has achieved the Annual Milestones, the term of the Agreement shall be automatically renewed for an additional five (5) years. Thereafter, at the end of each renewal term, the Agreement shall renew for an additional two (2) years unless one party provides the other party with prior written notice of non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Term and Termination 11.1 This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a period of five (5) years from the Reseller's first purchase order for Product issued to Todos (the "Initial Term"), unless terminated earlier by one of the parties in accordance with the terms of this Section 11. Upon completion of the Initial Term, regardless of whether the Reseller has achieved the Annual Milestones, the term of the Agreement shall not be automatically renewed for an additional five (5) years. Thereafter, at the end of each renewal term, the Agreement shall renew for an additional two (2) years unless one party provides the other party with prior written notice of non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 11.1 states that the agreement will be automatically renewed for an additional five years if the Reseller has achieved the Annual Milestones. By changing the text to 'regardless of whether the Reseller has achieved the Annual Milestones, the term of the Agreement shall not be automatically renewed for an additional five (5) years', the renewal is explicitly denied even if the milestones are met. This is a direct contradiction of the previous sentence, creating uncertainty about the term length and requirements for renewal in Section 11.1.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Term and Termination 11.1 This Agreement shall be effective as of the Effective Date and shall continue in effect for a period of five (5) years from the Reseller's first purchase order for Product issued to Todos (the "Initial Term"), unless terminated earlier by one of the parties in accordance with the terms of this Section 11. Upon completion of the Initial Term, regardless of whether the Reseller has achieved the Annual Milestones, the term of the Agreement shall not be automatically renewed for an additional five (5) years. Thereafter, at the end of each renewal term, the Agreement shall renew for an additional two (2) years unless one party provides the other party with prior written notice of non-renewal at least sixty (60) days prior to the expiration of the then-current term.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 11.1 directly contradicts the possibility of automatic renewal even if annual milestones are achieved. The original text stated that renewal would occur if milestones were met, but the changed text explicitly denies this possibility regardless of milestone achievement. The changed text and contradicted text is identical, the change in text directly contradicts with the meaning implied in the original text and introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
